Auto-IDAES competition.title: Indy Autonomous Challenge - Wikipedia
Auto-IDAES competition.snippet: 1 month ago - The first race took place in October 2021 on the Indianapolis Motor Speedway (IMS), after an initial three-year period of simulator-only challenges, which started in November 2019 as a proving ground to allow competing teams to develop and demonstrate the ability to race autonomously before receiving the physical race car. Since then, the competition has raced on several notable oval circuits such as Las Vegas Motor Speedway and Texas Motor Speedway, and in June 2023 in its first road course circuit, at the Monza Circuit.

Auto-IDAES competition.title: Overview - Indy Autonomous Challenge
Auto-IDAES competition.snippet: A primary goal of the IAC is to advance technology that can speed the commercialization of fully autonomous vehicles and deployments of advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS). These enhancements will lead to increased safety and performance in all modes of motorsports and commercial transportation. In addition, the competition is a platform for students to excel in STEM.

Auto-IDAES competition.title: AutoDrive Challenge™ II
Auto-IDAES competition.snippet: We just finished Year 3 of the four year competition. Year 1 focused on preparing, designing, and implementing software destined for testing on the physical car. The work done in Year 1 gave teams the foundation for Years 2-4. Year 2 had a focus on interface development on the Chevy Bolt EUV.
